name: Fidelity Mortgage Lenders, Inc.
description_short: Fidelity Mortgage Lenders specializes in non-consumer business-purpose loans secured by commercial and residential property, including hard money and trust deed investments.
description_long:
Fidelity Mortgage Lenders, Inc. is a California-based lending company licensed as a Real Estate Broker (CABRE #00388229) with corporate NMLS ID #1726526, operating from Los Angeles. The company positions itself as a lender for non-consumer, business-purpose loans rather than traditional consumer mortgages, focusing on borrowers with specific financing needs outside conventional banking channels.

The company offers three primary loan products: Commercial Real Estate Loans for business property financing, Residential Loans structured as business-purpose financing, and Trust Deed Investments for investors seeking alternative investment vehicles. Their marketing emphasizes hard money lending for distressed properties and value-add real estate acquisitions, positioning these as alternatives to traditional bank financing. The company maintains an active blog discussing topics like refinancing into hard money and purchasing distressed properties.

Fidelity differentiates itself through speed and flexibility in underwriting, marketing "Where Yes Means Done" as their tagline, and explicitly targeting investors and developers who need financing outside traditional bank parameters. They offer a refer-a-friend program and feature testimonials and deal promotions on their website, suggesting a relationship-based business model.

A critical caveat is that the website content accessed was a 404 error page, providing limited current operational details. The company's focus on non-consumer, business-purpose loans means they do not serve typical homebuyers seeking primary residence financing. Their hard money lending model typically carries higher rates and shorter terms than conventional mortgages, making them suitable only for investors comfortable with commercial lending terms.
pros: Licensed California Real Estate Broker with corporate NMLS registration (1726526) providing regulatory oversight
Specialized in hard money and business-purpose lending, offering speed and flexibility alternatives to traditional banks
Focus on distressed and value-add property financing, serving investors underserved by conventional lenders
Multiple loan product lines including commercial real estate, residential business-purpose loans, and trust deed investments
Established referral program and customer testimonials indicating repeat business relationships
Direct contact channels with dedicated phone line (800-752-9533) and email support
Transparent about business-purpose lending structure rather than masking terms
cons: Website contains a 404 error page, raising questions about current operational status and digital presence
Exclusively serves non-consumer, business-purpose loans—not suitable for homebuyers seeking primary residence financing
Hard money lending typically carries significantly higher interest rates and shorter loan terms than conventional mortgages
Limited publicly available information on current loan products, rates, terms, or underwriting criteria
No online loan application system accessible from provided website URL
best_for: Real estate investors seeking fast financing for distressed property acquisitions
Developers and builders needing bridge or construction financing outside traditional banking
Commercial property owners requiring business-purpose refinancing or acquisition loans
Trust deed investors seeking alternative investment vehicles with secured property backing
diagnosis: Fidelity Mortgage Lenders serves real estate investors and developers needing fast, flexible financing for business-purpose property acquisitions outside conventional banking channels. The critical caveat is that this is a hard money lender serving commercial/investment borrowers, not a consumer mortgage lender for primary residence purchases, and borrowers should expect higher rates and shorter terms typical of bridge and commercial lending.
